text:
Assistant: Important correction to my earlier theory: vector retrieval *did* surface real grounding content — `docs/product-spec/tail-ux.md` ("Anatomy of a line"), `tail-system.md` (filtering & rendering). Those committed specs ARE the "tail tui." So content exists, yet the gate still bailed. Let me capture what the select model actually received and returned. [...] - **Retrieval worked.** Vector search surfaced the right docs at the top: `docs/product-spec/tail-system.md` (0.52), `docs/product-spec/tail-ux.md` (0.43). `tail-ux.md` literally has an "Anatomy of a line" section with the gutter/rendering layout. **The grounding content for "tail tui" exists, is committed, is in the catalog, and was retrieved.**
- **The gate (`deepseek-v4-flash`) threw it away** — logged output was exactly `NOTHING_RELEVANT`.
